title: leptaulus citrioides f. boivinianus
intro: Leptaulus citrioides f. boivinianus, commonly known as the 'Boivin's Lemon-scented Tea Tree', is a fragrant and medicinal plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae. It is valued for its antimicrobial properties, which are used to treat various health issues, such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Leptaulus citrioidus f. boivinianus include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off fungal and bacterial infections, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and fever. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations, such as teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
